conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child. I have one child - Alex, who's 13 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me ask you a few questions to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

First, are you or your partner responsible for Alex? By this I mean, does Alex live with you and do you pay towards their upbringing (such as food, clothes, and other costs)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. Alex lives with me and I'm responsible for all their upbringing costs - you know, food, clothes, school things, all of that. I take care of everything they need.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Now I need to ask about your residency status.

Are you currently living in the UK, and do you have the right to reside here (for example, are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, or another immigration status that allows you to live in the UK)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester. And yes, I have the right to live here, no issues with that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect. One final question - is Alex currently in hospital or in local authority care for an extended period, or do they live with you full-time?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with me full-time, no hospital or care situations.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me just review the official eligibility criteria to confirm everything before I give you my final assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for your patience. Based on the information you've provided and the official UK government guidance, I can now make my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 13 years old (under 16)
- Alex lives with you full-time
- You're responsible for all of Alex's upbringing costs
- You live in the UK with the right to reside
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is not in hospital or care

You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit through the government website or by contacting the Child Benefit Office.

Please note: If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you would still be eligible to claim.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old (under 16), lives with you full-time, and you are responsible for their upbringing by paying for food, clothes, and other costs. You live in the UK and have the right to reside here. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and Alex is not in hospital or care. According to the official eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You're usually considered responsible if you live with them and/or pay towards their upbringing. All these criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for their 13-year-old child Alex. They are responsible for Alex who lives with them full-time, they cover all upbringing costs, they live in the UK with the right to reside, no one else is claiming for Alex, and there are no hospital or care circumstances that would affect eligibility.
